title: Rich Shattering Syrian Phyllo Bake
description: A romantic golden pastry featuring layers of paper-thin dough and a savory-sweet filling of blanched greens and aromatic honey. This dish offers a delicate crunch with a sophisticated flavor profile suitable for heart-healthy diets.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and grease a rectangular baking dish with melted butter.
Blanch the spinach in boiling water for sixty seconds then immediately shock it in an ice bath.
Squeeze all excess moisture from the spinach and chop it finely before placing it in a mixing bowl.
Stir the yogurt, walnuts, lemon zest, and cinnamon into the spinach until the mixture is uniform.
Layer eight sheets of phyllo dough into the pan while brushing each individual layer generously with melted butter.
Spread the spinach and yogurt mixture evenly over the top of the eighth phyllo sheet.
Drizzle two tablespoons of honey over the filling to add a subtle floral sweetness.
Cover the filling with the remaining eight sheets of phyllo dough while continuing to butter every layer.
Score the top layers of the pastry into diamond shapes using a very sharp knife.
Bake the pastry for thirty-five minutes until the surface is deep golden brown and shattering to the touch.
Drizzle the final tablespoon of honey over the hot pastry immediately after removing it from the oven.
Allow the dish to rest for ten minutes before serving to ensure the layers set properly.
